data.title: Prospective Randomized, Placebo-controlled Clinical Study to Study the Effect of Postoperative Additive Systemic Steroids in Chronic Rhinosinusitis With Nasal Polyps
data.brief_summary: This study investigate the additive effect of systemic postoperative steroids in patients with chronic rhinosinusitis (CRSwNP). All patients receive a functional endoscopic sinus surgery (FESS) followed by topical steroid spray for 3 months. Postoperatively, patients will be randomized to either an additional systemic steroid or a placebo for 1 month. Patients will be followed for 2 years. Effect on Nasal Polyp score (NPS), Lund-Kennedy-Score (LKS), recurrence rates, smell scores, Rhinosinusitis
data.eligibility_criteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* CRSwNP
* refractory to medical therapy
* no previous sinus surgery
* Lund-Kennedy-Score ≥ 1
* Lund-Mackay-Score ≥ 10

Exclusion Criteria:

* ciliary impairment
* autoimmune disease
* cystic fibrosis
* immunodeficiency

data.title: A Phase III, Randomized, Multicenter, Double-blind, Placebo-controlled Clinical Trial of Omalizumab in Patients With Chronic Rhinosinusitis With Nasal Polyps
data.country: United Kingdom, Canada, Germany, United States, Portugal, Mexico, Czechia, Russia, Ukraine, Poland
data.brief_summary:
The purpose of this study is to determine the efficacy and safety of omalizumab compared with placebo in adult participants with chronic rhinosinusitis with nasal polyps (CRSwNP) who have had an inadequate response to standard-of-care treatments.

Study GA39855 (POLYP 2; NCT03280537) was another Phase III study by the Sponsor with identical objectives and design and was run in parallel with this study.
data.eligibility_criteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Age 18-75 years, inclusive, at time of signing Informed Consent Form.
* Ability to comply with the study protocol, in the investigator's judgment.
* Nasal polyp score (NPS) \>= 5, with a unilateral score of \>= 2 for each nostril, at screening (Day -35), and on Day -7.
* Sino-
endpoints: Change From Baseline in Nasal Polyp Score (NPS) at Week 24
Change From Baseline in Average Daily Nasal Congestion Score (NCS) at Week 24

data.title: An Open-label Study to Identify Molecular Markers of Steroid Resistance in Nasal Polyposis Before and Following Treatment With Mometasone Furoate (MFNS) 2 Sprays/Nostril (100 mcg/Nostril) Twice Daily for 4 Weeks.
data.brief_summary:
Aim 1: To assess steroid sensitivity to mometasone furoate (MF) in cultured nasal polyp explant tissue in vitro.

Aim 2: To assess steroid sensitivity in vivo in each subject by comparing symptom scores, nasal endoscopic findings before and following 4 weeks of treatment with mometasone furoate nasal spray (MFNS) and by comparing tissue immunohistology in NP biopsies pre- and post-treatment withA MFNS.

Aim 3: To characterize the molecular signature of gene mRNA expression in "steroid-sensitive"

2. History of chronic rhinosinusitis (symptoms for at least 3 months). Subject must have two
endpoints: Change in Steroid Sensitivity in Vivo Symptom Scores - Trouble With Sense of Smell
Change in Steroid Sensitivity in Vivo Nasal Endoscopy Polyp Scores
